claim: Autologous transplantation of mesenchymal stem cells causes a higher rate of opportunistic infections than induction therapy with anti-interleukin-2 receptor antibodies.

result.claim: Autologous transplantation of mesenchymal stem cells causes a higher rate of opportunistic infections than induction therapy with anti-interleukin-2 receptor antibodies.
result.reasoning: A randomized controlled trial (Paper ID: 10582939) directly refutes the claim. The study, which compared autologous mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) induction therapy with anti-IL-2 receptor antibody induction therapy in renal transplant patients, found a significantly *decreased* risk of opportunistic infections in the MSC-treated groups compared to the control group (hazard ratio, 0.42; 95% CI, 0.20-0.85, P=.02). The conclusion explicitly states that the use of autologous MSCs resulted in a "decreased risk of opportunistic infection" compared to anti-IL-2 receptor antibody induction therapy. While the paper's author and year information are not available, and it has no citations, the study design (randomized controlled trial) provides strong evidence.

evidence.text: Autospecific CD4(+) T cells showed increased Th2 cytokine responses in the absence of IL-12.
evidence.source: Dual role of the IL-12/IFN-gamma axis in the development of autoimmune myocarditis: induction by IL-12 and protection by IFN-gamma.

evidence.text: Autophagy is a recently recognized immune effector mechanism against intracellular pathogens.
evidence.source: T helper 2 cytokines inhibit autophagic control of intracellular Mycobacterium tuberculosis.

evidence.text: Delayed T cell recovery and restricted T cell receptor diversity after allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation are associated with increased risks of infection and cancer relapse.
evidence.source: Quantitative assessment of T-cell repertoire recovery after hematopoietic stem cell transplantation

evidence.text: Fifty-one patients in the control group received anti-IL-2 receptor antibody plus standard-dose CNIs.
evidence.source: Induction therapy with autologous mesenchymal stem cells in living-related kidney transplants: a randomized controlled trial.

evidence.text: The presence of an autoimmune disease-associated IL2RA haplotype correlates with diminished IL-2 responsiveness in Ag-experienced CD4(+) T cells, as measured by phosphorylation of STAT5a.
evidence.source: Type 1 diabetes-associated IL2RA variation lowers IL-2 signaling and contributes to diminished CD4+CD25+ regulatory T cell function.

evidence.text: The presence of an autoimmune disease-associated IL2RA haplotype is associated with a reduction in the ability of Tregs to suppress proliferation of autologous effector T cells.
evidence.source: Type 1 diabetes-associated IL2RA variation lowers IL-2 signaling and contributes to diminished CD4+CD25+ regulatory T cell function.

evidence.text: Patients were inoculated with marrow-derived autologous MSC (1-2 x 10(6)/kg) at kidney reperfusion and two weeks later.
evidence.source: Induction therapy with autologous mesenchymal stem cells in living-related kidney transplants: a randomized controlled trial.

evidence.text: IL-12/IFN-gamma (Th1) axis was investigated in the development of autoimmune myocarditis.

evidence.text: IL-4 and IL-13 abrogated autophagy-mediated killing of intracellular mycobacteria in murine and human macrophages.

evidence.text: Immunotoxicity and autoimmunity are emerging as side effects of immunotherapy.

evidence.text: T cell activation is predicated on the interaction between the T cell receptor and peptide-major histocompatibility ligands.

evidence.text: The antibody response to TG2 bears signs of a primary immune response despite the disease chronicity.

evidence.text: Small-vessel vasculitis (SVV) is linked to antineutrophil cytoplasm autoantibodies (ANCAs).

evidence.text: Expansion of host macrophages prevented the development of alveolar proteinosis in mice transplanted with GM-Csf-receptor-deficient progenitors.
evidence.source: Tissue-resident macrophages self-maintain locally throughout adult life with minimal contribution from circulating monocytes.

evidence.text: CD4(+)CD25(+) regulatory T cells (Tregs) from individuals with a range of human autoimmune diseases are deficient in their ability to control autologous proinflammatory responses when compared with nondiseased, control individuals.

evidence.text: Mucosal autoantibodies to transglutaminase 2 (TG2) are generated in response to gluten in individuals who express human leukocyte antigen HLA-DQ2 or HLA-DQ8.
evidence.source: High abundance of plasma cells secreting transglutaminase 2–specific IgA autoantibodies with limited somatic hypermutation in celiac disease intestinal lesions

evidence.text: After 6 months, 4 of 53 patients (7.5%) in the autologous MSC plus standard-dose CNI group had biopsy-confirmed acute rejection.
evidence.source: Induction therapy with autologous mesenchymal stem cells in living-related kidney transplants: a randomized controlled trial.
